description: P2 Architecture - Port Spec Revision Repository for audit trail + extract generation service layer
steps: 1. Create a new DB migration lib/db/migrations/0008_add_generation_history.sql that adds: (a) generation_runs table (id SERIAL PK, project_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES projects ON DELETE CASCADE, user_id INT REFERENCES users, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', provider TEXT, model TEXT, prompt_tokens INT, completion_tokens INT, duration_ms INT, error_message TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()); (b) project_revisions table (id SERIAL PK, project_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES projects ON DELETE CASCADE, user_id INT REFERENCES users, revision_type TEXT NOT NULL, payload JSONB NOT NULL, message TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()); Add indexes on project_id for both tables.

2. Add the new tables to the Drizzle schema: create lib/db/src/schema/generation_runs.ts and lib/db/src/schema/project_revisions.ts following the pattern in projects.ts. Export from lib/db/src/schema/index.ts.

3. Create artifacts/api-server/src/lib/generation-service.ts: extract the generation pipeline logic from routes/projects.ts into a class or set of functions. Include methods: startGeneration(), runClarification(), runPlanning(), runBuilding(), runValidation(), runRepair(). Each method should record a generation_run and project_revision entry.
4. Refactor artifacts/api-server/src/routes/projects.ts to use the new generation service. The route handlers become thin adapters that set up SSE, call the service, and forward events.
5. Add a GET /api/projects/:id/history endpoint that returns all project_revisions for a project (ordered by created_at DESC). Protected by ownership check.
6. Add a GET /api/projects/:id/runs endpoint that returns all generation_runs for a project. Protected by ownership check.
7. Update the test setup to include the new migration, and add basic tests for the history/runs endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Migration 0008 creates generation_runs and project_revisions tables with proper indexes
Drizzle schema includes new tables and exports them
Generation service encapsulates the pipeline logic outside route handlers
routes/projects.ts is significantly shorter (generation logic moved to service)
GET /api/projects/:id/history returns revisions for the project
GET /api/projects/:id/runs returns generation runs for the project
Generation creates revision entries when it completes (plan saved, files saved)
New tests for history/runs endpoints pass
findings: Per orchestrator guidance, steps 3 and 4 (full service extraction + route refactor) were scoped down to adding recording hooks at key points rather than fully extracting the 1000+ line generation pipeline into a separate service. The infrastructure (tables, service module, endpoints, recording) is all in place. A full extraction would be a separate large refactor. Postgres needed to be started with the .pgdata directory at /projects/sandbox/.pgdata for tests. The unused Provider type import in refinement.ts was cleaned up.
